zh.lifeSummary: William Marmion Lord of Checkenden是Robert Marmion 2nd Baron Marmion of Tamworth的兒子，於1155年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾塔沃斯 卡素。他和Mrs William MARMION至少有1個兒子。他去世於1220年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾肯登享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Marmion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Marmion Lord of Checkenden was born in 1155, in Tamworth Castle, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Robert Marmion 2nd Baron Marmion of Tamworth. He had at least 1 son with Mrs William MARMION. He died in 1220, in Checkendon, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé en 1220, à Checkendon, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Marmion Lord of Checkendenは1155年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアタムウオルス カストルでRobert Marmion 2nd Baron Marmion of Tamworthの息子として生まれた。彼はMrs William MARMIONとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1220年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアチェクエンドンで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Marmion Lord of Checkenden(은)는 1155년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 타워스 카스틀에서 Robert Marmion 2nd Baron Marmion of Tamworth의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Mrs William MARMION(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 체c켄던에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: William Marmion Lord of Checkenden родился в 1155 г. в местности тамворт кастл, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Robert Marmion 2nd Baron Marmion of Tamworth. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs William MARMION. Он умер в 1220 г. в местности чеккендон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
